mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative S&P 500 returns in > 55% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or positive returns occur in < 50% of months with payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 310, persistent inflationary pressures trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 310.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly expansion in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates sustained aggregate demand growth, which directly boosts equity earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when either series shows contraction.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are followed by positive S&P 500 returns in > 55% of triggered instances.
